what_this_pass_proved: Timer ramp 20→18→16→14→12 is smoother than the previous 25→20→18→15→12 — L1 at 20s is still learnable but no longer dead time, and the step sizes are more consistent.
Wrong dispatch penalty (2-4s depending on level, zero at L1) gives mistakes a felt consequence without punishing exploration during learning.
L5 threshold at 400 (down from 9999) is now clearable — the game has a reachable endgame instead of an impossible wall.
L4 keeping all 5 trucks (was removing Ambulance) feels correct — removing a truck type felt like a bug, not a difficulty increase.
Scoring formula (base + remaining seconds + streak × urgency) makes speed rewarding and scores predictable — fast play on high-urgency incidents pays more.
open_questions: Is the L1 penalty of zero too generous? Players may develop bad dispatch habits that L2's penalty then punishes unexpectedly.
Does the L5 threshold of 400 feel achievable on a first attempt, or does it require multiple runs to learn the scoring math?
Is the urgency multiplier visible enough to the player? If not, the scoring feels arbitrary.

bugs_fixed.description: L1 timer at 25s was too generous — incidents sat on screen with no urgency. Reduced to 20s.
Wrong dispatch had zero penalty — player could spam dispatch attempts with no consequence. Added level-scaled penalty (2-4s).
L5 threshold at 9999 was unreachable — effectively impossible to clear. Lowered to 400.
L4 removed Ambulance truck type — felt like a bug. Restored all 5 trucks.

known_limits: Timer ramp has not been validated against real play. The 20→12 range may still be too fast or too slow at the extremes.
Penalty scaling (2-4s) is a design hypothesis. May need per-level tuning after observation.
Scoring formula not yet surfaced to the player in a way that teaches the strategy.

auditor_results.notes: Timer ramp [20, 18, 16, 14, 12] — steps of 10%, 11%, 13%, 14%. All within smooth range. Penalty ramp [0, 2, 3, 3, 4] is monotonic.

next_pass_first_obligation: Observe whether the wrong-dispatch penalty at L2 feels fair or like a surprise punishment after L1's zero-penalty learning mode.
